channel.title: Security on After the Code
channel.description: Recent content in Security on After the Code

item.title: Amadey Malware: A Comparative Study of Static Detection vs Memory-Based Detection
item.description: Comparing static vs memory-based malware detection using Amadey, showing why runtime YARA rules outperform file-hash approaches.

item.title: The Visibility Principle: How Internal Vulnerability Visibility Shapes Remediation Behavior
item.description: How transparent internal vulnerability visibility drives remediation through accountability and deterrence without formal punishment.

item.title: Endpoint Security Evasion (2020–2025): From EDR Bypass to EDR Kill
item.description: A technical analysis of how BYOI, BYOVD, DLL hijacking, and service abuse shifted endpoint attacks from EDR bypass to EDR kill between 2020 and 2025.

item.title: SPOF in Cybersecurity: From History to Strategy, a Graph-Based Analysis
item.description: Graph-based analysis of Single Points of Failure in cybersecurity, using weighted path enumeration to identify critical infrastructure nodes.

item.title: Dicer Module Vulnerability Mitigation Guide: CVE-2022-24434
item.description: Mitigation guide for CVE-2022-24434 in the Dicer module affecting Multer and Express, with practical dependency chain fixes.

item.title: How I Managed Unmaintained Open Source with Gmail and Snyk Alerts
item.description: Automating Snyk vulnerability alert management with Google Apps Script and Gmail when official API access falls short.

item.title: Is Your Data in the Cat's Paws?
item.description: Analysis of the 2025 KakaoPay breach exposing 40M users' data, and why formal consent fails without AI-based DPIA and civic oversight.

item.title: There’s No Such Thing as a Free Lunch, But Security Was Free
item.description: The CVE system nearly collapsed in 2025. Who should fund public cybersecurity infrastructure when free-riding is no longer sustainable?

item.title: Common Security Myths Developers Tell Themselves
item.description: Debunking developer security myths around responsibility deflection, tech overconfidence, and risk underestimation with real-world examples.

item.title: Can Development Culture Influence Security Levels?
item.description: Exploring how development culture shapes code security, with practical examples using static analysis tools like Joern.
